sample_source: 477 real eligible test-split cases (frontal + real findings + real impression)

generation_settings.top_p: not set by this system -- Ollama default applied
generation_settings.repeat_penalty: not set by this system -- Ollama default applied
generation_settings.seed: not set by this system -- Ollama default applied
generation_settings.max_tokens: not set by this system -- Ollama default applied

metrics_tier2.num_layers_note: not in bert_score's tuned model2layers table (general-domain checkpoints only); layer 9 chosen by matching architecture depth to bert-base-uncased's tuned choice, not independently re-validated for this biomedical model
metrics_tier2.environment: isolated venv (ml/evaluation/.venv-bertscore, torch==2.6.0, transformers==4.46.0) -- decoupled from the main .venv per Step 3's CVE-2025-32434 finding

validity_check.status: does not clear random-baseline validity bar
validity_check.random_baseline_method: mismatched-pairs derangement, seed 42, same model/layer/settings as real scoring
validity_check.rescaled_per_bertscore_paper_substitute: (x - random_baseline_mean) / (1 - random_baseline_mean), applied identically to real and baseline scores
validity_check.conclusion: Both raw and rescaled 95% CIs on the real-minus-random-baseline paired difference include zero for both fields -- real generated reports are not statistically distinguishable from mismatched pairs under this configuration. Rescaling is a positive affine transform applied identically to both sides of the paired difference, so it cannot change which side of zero the CI falls on -- this was mathematically guaranteed, not just empirically observed. Not pursued further (different layer/model) per this project's standing discipline against re-testing configurations until one passes (cf. Phase 0 Gate 2).
